sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: VfB Stuttgart, currently sitting at 10th in the league with 44 points, hosts FC Augsburg, who are just one position and one point behind at 11th with 43 points. This Bundesliga clash at the MHP Arena in Stuttgart promises to be a tight encounter between two mid-table sides. With both teams separated by just a single point, the stakes are high for securing a better standing in the league table. The referee for this match, Timo Gerach, averages 3.9 yellow cards per game, which could influence the physicality of this matchup. Additionally, Gerach has a red card average of 0.19 per game, suggesting a possibility of strict officiating.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head History
sections.text: Historically, VfB Stuttgart have dominated their recent encounters with FC Augsburg, winning 4 out of the last 5 matchups, with 1 draw and no wins for Augsburg. In their most recent meeting on February 4, 2025, Stuttgart secured a 1-0 victory at home, with Maximilian Mittelstädt earning Player of the Match honors with an impressive 8.0 Sofascore rating. Stuttgart have also kept a clean sheet in 4 of their last head-to-head games against Augsburg, showcasing their defensive solidity in this fixture. Furthermore, in 4 of the last 5 meetings, the total goals have been under 2.5, indicating tight, low-scoring affairs. Stuttgart have been the first to score in 4 of these 5 matches, often setting the tone early.

sections.subtitle: VfB Stuttgart’s Form and Key Players
sections.text: VfB Stuttgart have scored 57 goals this season, placing them 5th in the league for total goals scored, and they rank 3rd for big chances created with 104. Their attacking prowess is further highlighted by ranking 4th in average ball possession at 57.31% and 3rd in successful final third passes per game at 103. Players like Angelo Stiller, with an average Sofascore rating of 7.4 and 8 assists, have been pivotal, and he’s in fine form with a 7.94 rating over the last 5 matches. Ermedin Demirović, with 13 goals and a recent form rating of 7.06, is another key threat up front. Deniz Undav, a standout in past matchups against Augsburg with an average rating of 7.7 and 3 goals, will look to make an impact again. However, Stuttgart are missing midfielder Enzo Millot, who has contributed 5 goals and 4 assists this season, which could affect their midfield creativity.

sections.subtitle: FC Augsburg’s Form and Key Players
sections.text: FC Augsburg have struggled against Stuttgart recently, with no wins in their last 5 head-to-head matches and a current streak of 3 games without a victory overall. Their defense has been leaky in this fixture, failing to keep a clean sheet in their last 10 encounters with Stuttgart. On the positive side, players like Keven Schlotterbeck stand out with a league rank of 2 for ground duels won at 71.7%, providing defensive grit. Chrislain Matsima also ranks highly, 5th in the league for aerial duels won at 70.3%, which could be crucial against Stuttgart’s attack. Up front, Alexis Claude-Maurice leads with 9 goals, significantly overperforming his expected goals of 4.25, while Samuel Essende has chipped in with 7 goals. Frank Onyeka, ranked 2nd in the league for times fouled per game at 2.5, could draw fouls in dangerous areas.

sections.subtitle: Interesting Tactical Stats
sections.text: VfB Stuttgart excel in creating opportunities, ranking 4th in penalty area entries per game at 31.53 and 6th in possessions won in the final third per game at 4.03, suggesting strong pressing and attacking intent. They also score heavily late in games, with 29.41% of their goals coming between the 76th and 90th minutes. In contrast, FC Augsburg distribute their goals more evenly across match periods, with peaks of 22.22% in both the 1-15 and 31-45 minute brackets. Augsburg’s defense will need to be wary of Stuttgart’s 104 big chances created, as the home side ranks 3rd in the league for this metric. However, Stuttgart’s conversion rate of big chances stands at just 35.58%, ranking 14th, which could give Augsburg some hope if they defend tightly.

sections.subtitle: Disciplinary and Set-Piece Insights
sections.text: Discipline could play a role in this match, with referee Timo Gerach averaging 3.9 yellow cards per game, and Stuttgart having accumulated 68 yellow cards this season, ranking 12th for least yellow cards. Both teams have a trend of matches with less than 4.5 cards, with Stuttgart at 5 out of 7 recent games and Augsburg at 7 out of 7. In terms of set-pieces, Stuttgart average 5.03 corners per game, ranking 4th in the league, while head-to-head matches between these teams have seen less than 10.5 corners in 4 of the last 5 encounters. Stuttgart’s aerial presence is moderate, with 7 headers scored ranking them 11th, while Augsburg will rely on defenders like Chrislain Matsima to win key aerial duels.

sections.subtitle: Notable Individual Performances
sections.text: For Stuttgart, Maximilian Mittelstädt has been a standout with 6 assists and a Sofascore rating of 7.34, and his Player of the Match performance in the last meeting with Augsburg (8.0 rating) makes him one to watch. Young defender Finn Jeltsch also impresses for Stuttgart, ranking 5th in the league for ground duels won at 69.4%, showing defensive tenacity. For Augsburg, Jeffrey Gouweleeuw provides experience in defense with 4 assists and a rating of 7.09, while Dimitrios Giannoulis has contributed 4 assists from midfield with a rating of 7.0. Lesser-known names like Stuttgart’s Nick Woltemade, with 10 goals, and Augsburg’s Phillip Tietz, with 6 goals, could also play decisive roles in this matchup.
